items.prompt: X:\YesAndCattery local supabase/migrations/ has diverged from the live Supabase project emrerjdyujylgutfmool. Three migrations are APPLIED IN PROD but have NO FILE in the repo: `reviews_and_ratings` (version 20260630190120, the spec's cited "0009"), `waitlists` (20260630233349, the "0010"), and `admin_set_flags_self_lockout_guard` (20260630183939, a 0007-era guard). Conversely `0006_photos_storage_bucket` exists as a repo file but is NOT in the prod migration history (the photos bucket exists live, created out-of-band via the dashboard). Impact: (a) a fresh `supabase db push` from the repo builds a DB with no `reviews` and no `waitlist_entries` tables, then 0012_notification_resolver.sql line 34 joins public.waitlist_entries and FAILS - the schema is not reproducible from source; (b) the RLS policies protecting reviews (77 live rows, review authorship) and waitlist_entries (26 live rows, buyer PII) live only in prod and cannot be reviewed or reproduced from version control. Live RLS is confirmed ON for both tables and get_advisors reports no missing-policy on them, so there is NO active breach - but two user-data tables have an unversioned security boundary and the repo can't rebuild prod. FIX: use the Supabase MCP (execute_sql against pg_catalog / information_schema, or `supabase db diff`) to pull the exact live DDL for the three missing migrations INCLUDING their RLS policies, and commit them as correctly-numbered migration files that reproduce prod. Reconcile 0006_photos_storage_bucket (either record it in the migration history or fold the storage.objects bucket+policy into a tracked migration). Verify a clean rebuild does not fail at 0012. Then update PROJECT_SPEC.md line ~114 'Files at a glance' migration bullet (currently cites nonexistent 0009/0010 and omits 0011-0016) to match the reconciled set. NOT auto-safe: requires live-DDL extraction + judgment on numbering; edits the canonical doc. Ships via X:\YesAndCattery\scriptselease.ps1; schema files go through `supabase db push`, not the release script.

items.prompt: X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md section 8 Locked Decision 'PNG-only art, zero SVG in the label (v0.8.2)' ends with the absolute sentence 'No SVG anywhere in the rendered label.' (line ~303). That literal claim is contradicted by src/render.js, which emits inline SVG for structural chrome: the decorative dividers (divider-top/divider-bot, lines ~27/33), the parchment background (parchmentSvg, line ~48), and the border overlay (line ~135). The decision's intent (all symbol and illustration ART is PNG via data/symbols/ + data/illustrations/) is fully honored by the code; only the over-broad final sentence drifts. Section 4.4 line ~76 carries the same over-broad parenthetical ('zero SVG anywhere in the label'), though in context it clearly means symbol art. Reword to scope the claim to ART, e.g. section 8: 'No SVG art anywhere in the rendered label; the only SVG in the render tree is structural chrome (decorative dividers, parchment background, border overlay), never symbol or illustration art.' and optionally tighten section 4.4's parenthetical to 'zero SVG for symbol art'. Structural because it edits a Locked Decision's wording: needs Nick's sign-off (he may prefer to leave the shorthand, since the intent is clear and the code substance is aligned). Do NOT auto-apply; do NOT change the decision's substance. Ships via X:\YesAndApothecary\scripts\release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. items.prompt: Yes& Chains [HIGH, bar-raise finding auth-01]: the Cloudflare Worker verifies no Supabase JWT and trusts a client-supplied body.userId for every entitlement decision, so anyone who knows an entitled account's UUID can spoof Pro access to the paid AI-spend endpoints. Evidence: worker/src/lib.js:1717-1734 (_requireEntitledUser) and :1678-1701 (_checkSubscriptionEntitled) read body.userId with only a shape check; worker/src/handlers/billing.js:53,446,566,617 and contribute.js:290-297,365-372,403-410 do the same. Fix: require the Supabase access-token in an Authorization header on the billing, AI-spend, and contribution routes, verify it (Supabase /auth/v1/user or JWT signature), and derive userId from the verified claim instead of the request body. Live paid product, so treat as pre-1.0. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1; app.js is build output, edit src/ + worker/src/. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: Enqueued by bar-raise-yac 2026-07-04. New HIGH this run; not in prior openFindings.

Fix: restore legal/terms.md to a complete document and reconcile it with the shipped TERMS_MD so counsel reviews the same text users see. Use tools/safe_write.py for the canonical-doc write (FUSE truncation guard). Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Verify current line count first; this pairs with compliance-02/03 (add a payment/refund section + drop the DRAFT banner).

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding install-offline-01]: a starred course opened offline renders only a par-3 skeleton because canonical hole/pin/map detail lives in memory (state.courseCanonicals) and is re-fetched from the network-only worker API on boot, never persisted. Evidence: src/persistence.ts:162 re-fetches via fetchCanonicalCourse on boot; src/courses-helpers.ts:116-117 stores canonical in in-memory state only; sw.js:70-74,105 treats /api/courses/* as network-only, contradicting the sw.js:6-8 comment about offline coverage. Fix: persist state.courseCanonicals for starred courses to localStorage (or IndexedDB) on fetch and hydrate on boot, and correct the sw.js header comment. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: Enqueued by bar-raise-yac 2026-07-04. New MED this run; offline UX correctness.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding finance-03]: checkout creation neither blocks an already-active subscriber nor sends a Stripe idempotency key, so a double-tap or a re-opened upgrade modal can create a second live subscription on the same customer and double-bill the user. Evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:566-604 (handleBillingCheckout) has no pre-check of subscription_status and no Idempotency-Key; src/subscription.ts:158 openUpgradeModal() is window-exposed with no entitled-guard. Fix: before creating a Checkout Session, load the profile and short-circuit if subscription_status is active/trialing, and pass an Idempotency-Key keyed by userId+plan on the /checkout/sessions call. Live payments, so pre-1.0. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: Enqueued by bar-raise-yac 2026-07-04. New MED this run; double-bill risk.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding sync-01]: bags, discs, and collection use replace-on-load plus blind delete-missing upsert (last-write-wins, no timestamp guard), unlike rounds which union-merge with tombstones, so a disc added on device A while device B edits offline can be deleted when B syncs. Evidence: src/cloud.ts:673 (cloudLoadBagsAndDiscs wholesale-replaces state.bags), :855-883 (cloudSyncBagsAndDiscs full-set upsert + delete-missing, no guard) vs :1098-1112 (roundHistory id-keyed union merge). Fix: give bags/discs/collection the same id-keyed union-merge-with-tombstones treatment rounds already use, or add an updatedAt guard so a stale device cannot delete rows it never loaded. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: Enqueued by bar-raise-yac 2026-07-04. New MED this run; cross-device data loss.

items.prompt: YaB MED (bar-raise 2026-07-04): POST /api/transactions/bulk-delete (apps/api/src/server.ts:534-544) permanently deletes transactions with no pre-delete snapshot, the only user-facing destructive path that skips the createBackup step every other mutating route takes (import-pipeline.ts:70/188, server.ts:1411/1425 plaid takeover/restore). Add createBackup("pre-bulk-delete") at the top of the handler and return 503 backup_failed on snapshot failure, mirroring the import/rollback/takeover pattern; add a regression test. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

YaB MED (bar-raise 2026-07-04): scripts/push-to-github.ps1:28-31 still blind-deletes .git\index.lock (Remove-Item -Force) before its first Assert-GitSafe at line 56, the exact unguarded FUSE-corruption race git-guard.ps1 exists to prevent. v0.14.4 moved release.ps1 (guarded Remove-StaleGitLock :57) and preship.ps1 (:24) to the guarded path but push-to-github.ps1 was missed, and release.ps1 delegates its push to it (:228). CLAUDE.md Hazards claims the blind-delete paths are gone, now inaccurate for this script. Delete the lines-28-31 block and rely on the existing Assert-GitSafe (git-guard already dot-sourced at :19). Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

YaB MED (bar-raise 2026-07-04): the CORS allow-list (apps/api/src/server.ts:90-99) grants https://yesandeverything.com on /* (every route) though the YaE landing page only probes GET /api/stats to detect the local API. Scope the yesandeverything.com origin to the GET / or /api/stats probe route only, keeping localhost:5173 as the sole origin on data + mutation endpoints. Low-friction hardening (no UX cost; YaE only calls the probe). The separate per-boot-token / Sec-Fetch-Site guard on destructive endpoints stays deferred pending Nick's friction-level call. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
